title: Buffer Management for SMTP Security Drivers
breadcrumbs: Buffer Management for SMTP Security Drivers
text: Buffer Management for SMTP Security Drivers When establishing or removing a security context, drivers prepare messages and receive replies of varying lengths. The caller provides static buffers for input and output, which the driver can use. If either buffer is too small for the purposes of the driver, the driver can allocate larger buffers and use those instead. When a driver returns a status of USEC_INFO and USEC_ERROR , it should return an explanatory message pointed to by the ErrMsg member. The message is used only if the length of the buffer in ErrMsgLen member is not zero. This allows a pointer to allocate memory to be stored in ErrMsg ,without it necessarily being printed. The driver is completely responsible for the memory it uses. The caller of the driver interface will not attempt to modify the message, but the message must be null-terminated. This leaves the driver free to use fixed strings or an allocated buffer. With the exception of an error in the USEC_DRVINFO call, the driver will always be called finally with the USEC_CLOSE function code, at which time it can free any allocated resources. If an error occurs in the USEC_DRVINFO call, the driver will not be called again and should have freed any memory it has allocated. Related Links USEC_OPENMSG Prepares the messages that are to be sent to the server in order to carry out the initialisation and authentication protocol to establish a connection. USEC_DRVINFO Determines the function codes that are valid for this driver. USEC_CLOSE Driver is responsible for releasing any resources it acquired. Security Context A security context is the complete security environment required for a secure connection to a mail server. It consists of one or more security drivers that are responsible for connection and authentication to the email server. Security Drivers for Email Authentication Uniface provides security drivers for several SASL (Simple Authentication and Security Layer) mechanisms for SMTP and POP authentication. These can be used by the Uniface UPOPMAIL component to set a security context when sending and receiving mail. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
